activities_projects.title: Helping Someone Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Encourage students to reflect on acts of kindness
steps: Recall a time when you helped someone
Write two words describing how you felt after helping
Write what the other person might have felt
Share your responses with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the emotional value of helping others
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of reflection and participation in sharing (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Word Association Activity
steps: Read the list of words given in the activity
Circle the words that describe an elderly person
Underline the words that describe a school student
Discuss reasons for your choices with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to associate vocabulary with appropriate contexts

activities_projects.title: Understanding Word Meaning
steps: Read the line describing the woman as 'ragged'
Review the different meanings of the word
Select the correct meaning used in the poem
Discuss the reason with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn contextual meanings of words

activities_projects.title: Poem Event Sequencing
activities_projects.objective: Understand the sequence of events in the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
List the events described in the poem
Arrange them in the correct order
Discuss the sequence with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop comprehension and sequencing skills

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognise the central theme of the poem
steps: Read the poem again carefully
Identify the central message of the poem
Discuss whether the poem highlights kindness or adventure
Write your explanation in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of empathy and kindness

activities_projects.title: Poetic Devices Identification
activities_projects.objective: Identify poetic devices used in the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify examples of simile, alliteration, and repetition
Write the examples in your notebook
Discuss how they enhance the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn literary techniques used in poetry
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of poetic devices (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Imagery in Poetry
activities_projects.objective: Understand how imagery creates vivid scenes
steps: Read the descriptive lines in the poem
Identify phrases that create imagery
Explain how these phrases describe the street and weather
Share your explanation with the class

activities_projects.title: Character Comparison
steps: Compare the old woman and the young boy
Write qualities describing each character
Explain how the boy’s actions show empathy
Discuss your analysis with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand character traits and moral values
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Narrating a Helping Experience
steps: Think of a time when you helped someone
Describe what happened and how you helped
Explain how you felt afterward
Narrate your experience to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve communication and storytelling skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and confidence in narration (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Diary Entry Writing
steps: Think about a day when you helped someone
Write a diary entry describing the situation
Explain how you helped and how you felt
Conclude by reflecting on why helping others is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve personal writing and empathy
